value: {"cycleId":"f7cbb1bb-1f43-4f29-9103-1718f0c7c653","sprintVersion":"9.0.0","verdict":"APPROVE","rationale":"Sprint v9.0.0 delivers the core memory wiring objective end-to-end (schema, write paths in cycle/gate/review phases, read paths in audit/execute, dashboard surface, and integration test) with 18/20 items completed and strong test coverage. Code reviewer issued APPROVE with only one MAJOR (hardcoded import.meta.url paths in flywheel.ts — latent, not live) and minor structural nits. The two failed items (/approvals wiring and /branches tab) are isolated dashboard routes that don't block the memory layer shipping and can be re-queued for v9.1 alongside the reviewer's cleanup suggestions. Cost ($15.61) is well within envelope.","criticalFindings":["\"response\": \"`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`\\n**The memory type schism** fixed in `execute-phase.ts` is a classic API evolution hazard — when writers use `id` (canonical UUID) and readers expect `key` (human-readable slug), the labels silently render as `undefined` in prompts. The broadened local interface (`id?: string; key?: string`) is the right fix: both shapes parse cleanly and `key ?? id ?? type` always resolves a non-empty label. Re-exporting a stricter external type (`CycleMemoryEntry` with `id: string` required) would have made test fixtures that only supply `key` fail at the type-checker — keeping the type local-and-loose avoids that brittleness.\\n`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Code Review: AgentForge Sprint v9.0.0 / commit `feat(v6.7.4)`\\n\\n### What This Change Is\\n\\nA \\\"salvage\\\" commit that cherry-picks the cross-cycle memory layer from a gate-rejected cycle (`ee9580bd`) plus two focused bug fixes (MemoryEntry type schism, `approvalsRoutes` signature), adds a new `/api/v5/flywheel` route, refactors the approvals dashboard to cycle-based UX, and adds ~330 lines of new tests.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Overall Correctness\\n\\nThe change does exactly what the commit message claims. The three thread counts line up:\\n\\n| Claimed | Delivered |\\n|---|---|\\n| Fix `type` key duplication in `logGitEvent` / `logPREvent` | ✅ Verified in diff |\\n| Revert `approvalsRoutes(app, opts)` → `approvalsRoutes(app)` | ✅ Confirmed |\\n| Memory wiring: `cycle-logger`, `audit-phase`, `gate-phase` | ✅ Salvaged cleanly |\\n| New `/api/v5/flywheel` endpoint with 4 gauges + `memoryStats` | ✅ Route registered, tested |\\n| Review phase writes `review-finding` memory entries per CRITICAL/MAJOR | ✅ Implemented and tested |\\n| Markdown code-fence guard in `proposal-to-backlog` | ✅ Implemented and tested (line 261 in test file) |\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Issues\\n\\n**MAJOR — Hardcoded absolute paths in `flywheel.ts` (not injection-safe, but fragile)**\\n```ts\\n// src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:8-18\\nconst MEMORY_JSONL_DIR = join(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../.agentforge/memory');\\nconst CYCLES_DIR = join(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../.agentforge/cycles');\\n```\\n`computeMemoryStats()` and `computeVelocity()` compute `projectRoot` from `import.meta.url`, while every other route receives it via options (e.g., `opts.adapter`, `opts.projectRoot`). If the build output tree changes or the server is started from a symlinked path, these silently resolve to the wrong directory. The functions should accept `projectRoot: string` as a parameter (or read it from opts), consistent with the pattern in `execute-phase.ts` and `review-phase.ts`.\\n\\n**MINOR — Silent error swallowing in both flywheel route catch blocks**\\n```ts\\n// Lines 443, 484\\n} catch {\\n  // returns zero-state — original error is silently dropped, not even console.warn'd\\n}\\n```\\nThe `v1` and `v5` catch blocks return valid zero-state objects, which is correct for UI resilience, but they log nothing. A `console.warn` or a Fastify `log.warn` call would help operators diagnose issues in production. Compare with the rest of the codebase which uses `console.warn` in similar fallback cases.\\n\\n**MINOR — `review-phase.ts` depends on `gate-phase.ts` via `extractFindingsByLevel`**\\n```ts\\nimport { extractFindingsByLevel } from './gate-phase.js';\\n```\\nReview runs *before* gate in the 9-phase pipeline, so this reverse import is an architectural smell. `extractFindingsByLevel` is a pure text-parsing utility and would be better placed in a shared `phase-utils.ts` or colocated in `review-phase.ts` itself. Not a bug, but it muddies the layering.\\n\\n**MINOR — Global approval store uses `var` throughout the IIFE**\\nThe new `initApprovalStore` IIFE in `dashboard/index.html` uses `var` declarations exclusively, which is inconsistent with the `const`/`let` used everywhere else in the same file. Pre-ES5 style in 2026 is fine for a legacy HTML file but signals the agent wasn't style-matching.\\n\\n**MINOR — `computeVelocity`/`computeInheritance` vs. `computeMetaLearning`/`computeAutonomy` — inconsistent signatures**\\nTwo helpers accept `adapter: SqliteAdapter` and two hit the filesystem independently. This makes unit-testing the filesystem-backed helpers harder (no injection point for a mock `projectRoot`). A minor structural inconsistency now but it will compound.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Security\\n\\nNo SQL injection, XSS, or credential exposure introduced. The approval modal uses `textContent` for all user-supplied values, and cycle IDs are `encodeURIComponent`-escaped before being included in fetch URLs. ✅\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Test Coverage\\n\\nCoverage for new functionality is solid:\\n- `flywheel-v5.test.ts` — 20+ contract + data-driven tests, including zero-state, cap validation, and score math.\\n- `phase-handlers-verification.test.ts` — 5 memory-persistence tests for review phase, including \\\"no entry when only MINOR findings.\\\"\\n- `proposal-to-backlog.test.ts` — Code-fence guard has a proper regression test at line 261.\\n- `sprint-framework.test.ts` — 15 lifecycle + gate-verdict memory integration tests.\\n\\n**Gap:** No test covers the case where `computeVelocity()` reads actual sprint files. The filesystem helpers are tested only via the HTTP contract (zero-state assertions), meaning a regression in the file-reading logic would not be caught until the endpoint returned wrong numbers.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Verdict\\n\\n```\\nVERDICT: APPROVE\\nSUMMARY: Solid salvage commit — memory wiring, flywheel gauges, and approvals UX all correct and well-tested; two minor structural issues (hardcoded paths, reverse import) should be addressed in a follow-up item\\nISSUES:\\n  - [MAJOR]  computeMemoryStats/computeVelocity use import.meta.url-relative paths instead of injected projectRoot — fragile if build layout changes (src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:8-18, 296)\\n  - [MINOR]  Both flywheel catch blocks swallow errors without logging (src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:443, 484)\\n  - [MINOR]  review-phase.ts imports extractFindingsByLevel from gate-phase.ts — reverse pipeline dependency (packages/core/src/autonomous/phase-handlers/review-phase.ts:8)\\n  - [MINOR]  Global approval store IIFE uses var throughout; inconsistent with rest of file (dashboard/index.html)\\n  - [MINOR]  computeVelocity/computeInheritance don't accept projectRoot parameter, making them untestable in isolation\\nSUGGESTIONS:\\n  - Move extractFindingsByLevel to a shared phase-utils.ts to break the review→gate import\\n  - Add a filesystem-seeded unit test for computeVelocity() to guard the sprint-file parsing logic\\n  - Add console.warn in the flywheel catch blocks for operator visibility\\n```\\n\\n**Overall: 4 / 5** — Ship it. The MAJOR path issue is a latent risk but not a live bug (the current build layout works), and the test coverage delta is strongly positive. The structural issues are good candidates for a v9.1 cleanup ticket.\""],"majorFindings":["\"response\": \"`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`\\n**The memory type schism** fixed in `execute-phase.ts` is a classic API evolution hazard — when writers use `id` (canonical UUID) and readers expect `key` (human-readable slug), the labels silently render as `undefined` in prompts. The broadened local interface (`id?: string; key?: string`) is the right fix: both shapes parse cleanly and `key ?? id ?? type` always resolves a non-empty label. Re-exporting a stricter external type (`CycleMemoryEntry` with `id: string` required) would have made test fixtures that only supply `key` fail at the type-checker — keeping the type local-and-loose avoids that brittleness.\\n`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Code Review: AgentForge Sprint v9.0.0 / commit `feat(v6.7.4)`\\n\\n### What This Change Is\\n\\nA \\\"salvage\\\" commit that cherry-picks the cross-cycle memory layer from a gate-rejected cycle (`ee9580bd`) plus two focused bug fixes (MemoryEntry type schism, `approvalsRoutes` signature), adds a new `/api/v5/flywheel` route, refactors the approvals dashboard to cycle-based UX, and adds ~330 lines of new tests.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Overall Correctness\\n\\nThe change does exactly what the commit message claims. The three thread counts line up:\\n\\n| Claimed | Delivered |\\n|---|---|\\n| Fix `type` key duplication in `logGitEvent` / `logPREvent` | ✅ Verified in diff |\\n| Revert `approvalsRoutes(app, opts)` → `approvalsRoutes(app)` | ✅ Confirmed |\\n| Memory wiring: `cycle-logger`, `audit-phase`, `gate-phase` | ✅ Salvaged cleanly |\\n| New `/api/v5/flywheel` endpoint with 4 gauges + `memoryStats` | ✅ Route registered, tested |\\n| Review phase writes `review-finding` memory entries per CRITICAL/MAJOR | ✅ Implemented and tested |\\n| Markdown code-fence guard in `proposal-to-backlog` | ✅ Implemented and tested (line 261 in test file) |\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Issues\\n\\n**MAJOR — Hardcoded absolute paths in `flywheel.ts` (not injection-safe, but fragile)**\\n```ts\\n// src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:8-18\\nconst MEMORY_JSONL_DIR = join(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../.agentforge/memory');\\nconst CYCLES_DIR = join(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../.agentforge/cycles');\\n```\\n`computeMemoryStats()` and `computeVelocity()` compute `projectRoot` from `import.meta.url`, while every other route receives it via options (e.g., `opts.adapter`, `opts.projectRoot`). If the build output tree changes or the server is started from a symlinked path, these silently resolve to the wrong directory. The functions should accept `projectRoot: string` as a parameter (or read it from opts), consistent with the pattern in `execute-phase.ts` and `review-phase.ts`.\\n\\n**MINOR — Silent error swallowing in both flywheel route catch blocks**\\n```ts\\n// Lines 443, 484\\n} catch {\\n  // returns zero-state — original error is silently dropped, not even console.warn'd\\n}\\n```\\nThe `v1` and `v5` catch blocks return valid zero-state objects, which is correct for UI resilience, but they log nothing. A `console.warn` or a Fastify `log.warn` call would help operators diagnose issues in production. Compare with the rest of the codebase which uses `console.warn` in similar fallback cases.\\n\\n**MINOR — `review-phase.ts` depends on `gate-phase.ts` via `extractFindingsByLevel`**\\n```ts\\nimport { extractFindingsByLevel } from './gate-phase.js';\\n```\\nReview runs *before* gate in the 9-phase pipeline, so this reverse import is an architectural smell. `extractFindingsByLevel` is a pure text-parsing utility and would be better placed in a shared `phase-utils.ts` or colocated in `review-phase.ts` itself. Not a bug, but it muddies the layering.\\n\\n**MINOR — Global approval store uses `var` throughout the IIFE**\\nThe new `initApprovalStore` IIFE in `dashboard/index.html` uses `var` declarations exclusively, which is inconsistent with the `const`/`let` used everywhere else in the same file. Pre-ES5 style in 2026 is fine for a legacy HTML file but signals the agent wasn't style-matching.\\n\\n**MINOR — `computeVelocity`/`computeInheritance` vs. `computeMetaLearning`/`computeAutonomy` — inconsistent signatures**\\nTwo helpers accept `adapter: SqliteAdapter` and two hit the filesystem independently. This makes unit-testing the filesystem-backed helpers harder (no injection point for a mock `projectRoot`). A minor structural inconsistency now but it will compound.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Security\\n\\nNo SQL injection, XSS, or credential exposure introduced. The approval modal uses `textContent` for all user-supplied values, and cycle IDs are `encodeURIComponent`-escaped before being included in fetch URLs. ✅\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Test Coverage\\n\\nCoverage for new functionality is solid:\\n- `flywheel-v5.test.ts` — 20+ contract + data-driven tests, including zero-state, cap validation, and score math.\\n- `phase-handlers-verification.test.ts` — 5 memory-persistence tests for review phase, including \\\"no entry when only MINOR findings.\\\"\\n- `proposal-to-backlog.test.ts` — Code-fence guard has a proper regression test at line 261.\\n- `sprint-framework.test.ts` — 15 lifecycle + gate-verdict memory integration tests.\\n\\n**Gap:** No test covers the case where `computeVelocity()` reads actual sprint files. The filesystem helpers are tested only via the HTTP contract (zero-state assertions), meaning a regression in the file-reading logic would not be caught until the endpoint returned wrong numbers.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Verdict\\n\\n```\\nVERDICT: APPROVE\\nSUMMARY: Solid salvage commit — memory wiring, flywheel gauges, and approvals UX all correct and well-tested; two minor structural issues (hardcoded paths, reverse import) should be addressed in a follow-up item\\nISSUES:\\n  - [MAJOR]  computeMemoryStats/computeVelocity use import.meta.url-relative paths instead of injected projectRoot — fragile if build layout changes (src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:8-18, 296)\\n  - [MINOR]  Both flywheel catch blocks swallow errors without logging (src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:443, 484)\\n  - [MINOR]  review-phase.ts imports extractFindingsByLevel from gate-phase.ts — reverse pipeline dependency (packages/core/src/autonomous/phase-handlers/review-phase.ts:8)\\n  - [MINOR]  Global approval store IIFE uses var throughout; inconsistent with rest of file (dashboard/index.html)\\n  - [MINOR]  computeVelocity/computeInheritance don't accept projectRoot parameter, making them untestable in isolation\\nSUGGESTIONS:\\n  - Move extractFindingsByLevel to a shared phase-utils.ts to break the review→gate import\\n  - Add a filesystem-seeded unit test for computeVelocity() to guard the sprint-file parsing logic\\n  - Add console.warn in the flywheel catch blocks for operator visibility\\n```\\n\\n**Overall: 4 / 5** — Ship it. The MAJOR path issue is a latent risk but not a live bug (the current build layout works), and the test coverage delta is strongly positive. The structural issues are good candidates for a v9.1 cleanup ticket.\""]}

{"cycleId":"bbcde323-e272-4da4-bf44-1b2836ae4cbc","sprintVersion":"9.0.1","verdict":"REJECT","rationale":"The sprint delivered strong feature breadth — 19 of 20 items completed, solid test additions, and good architectural patterns — but the CRITICAL gate-verdict bug is a non-negotiable blocker. The gate phase is the core self-correction mechanism of the autonomous loop: if REJECT verdicts are silently mis-tagged as APPROVE, the system loses its ability to learn from failures, which is the entire point of the memory-wiring sprint. Shipping this would embed a silent correctness regression into the learning loop that compounds over every subsequent cycle. The two meta.total pagination bugs further erode dashboard trust. Fix the three real bugs (CRITICAL + 2 MAJOR), re-run the gate-phase test to confirm REJECT propagation, and resubmit — the underlying work is sound and should merge cleanly once these are addressed.","criticalFindings":["\"response\": \"`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`\\nThe uncommitted changes show 28 files with 1,767 net new lines. Running tests against both states reveals the sprint **reduced total failures from 44 → 31** (13 pre-existing tests fixed), but introduced 1 new regression and carries two `meta.total` correctness bugs across the new filtered memory endpoints.\\n`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Code Review — Sprint v9.0.1\\n\\n### Overall Correctness\\n\\nThe sprint's stated goals (gate-verdict memory, memory endpoint filters, cross-cycle audit propagation, SSE v5 stream, v5 branch endpoints, DRY branch-route refactor) are all present and structurally sound. The execute-phase cap-D fix (`Math.min(itemCount, ceilingParallelism)`) is correct and the test was updated to match the new optimistic parallelism behavior. Most of the work is solid.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Issues\\n\\n**[CRITICAL] REJECT verdict mis-tagged as APPROVE — `packages/server/src/lib/phase-handlers.ts:661`**\\n\\nThe new `runGatePhase` reads verdict from the sprint file _after_ `runLlmPhase` completes. Since `runLlmPhase` appends a new `phaseResult` entry, `.pop()` returns the LLM phase's freshly-written result — not any seeded historical entry. In the test fixture this overwritten entry contains the mock agent's response (typically empty/approve-shaped), so a sprint seeded with `\\\"REJECT: test coverage too low\\\"` is silently tagged `verdict:approve`. This is confirmed by `gate-phase-memory.test.ts` failing with `expected ['verdict:approve', …] to include 'verdict:reject'`. The response should be read from the `RunResult` returned directly by `runLlmPhase`, not scraped from disk.\\n\\n**[MAJOR] `meta.total` reports paginated count, not total matches — `packages/server/src/routes/v5/dashboard-stubs.ts` and `src/server/routes/memory.ts`**\\n\\n```ts\\n// dashboard-stubs.ts\\nconst data = filtered.slice(0, pageLimit);\\nreturn reply.send({ data, meta: { total: data.length } }); // ← data.length is sliced\\n```\\n\\n`meta.total` is supposed to tell the client how many entries _match_ the filters before pagination. After `.slice(0, pageLimit)`, `data.length` ≤ `pageLimit`, not the true match count. Any UI that renders \\\"showing X of Y\\\" or drives infinite scroll will misbehave. Should be `filtered.length`.\\n\\n**[MINOR] `readGateVerdict()` is tightly coupled to internal file layout — `packages/core/src/autonomous/cycle-logger.ts:134`**\\n\\n`CycleLogger` now reaches directly into `cycles/<id>/phases/gate.json` to read structured verdict data. This dual-write path (phase handler writes gate.json → cycle logger re-reads it) creates an implicit ordering contract. If the gate phase ever writes under a different path or format, the method silently returns `null` with no warning. Passing the verdict explicitly through `logCycleResult`'s parameter struct would be safer.\\n\\n**[MINOR] Version hardcode stale in integration test — `tests/v5/server-integration.test.ts:22`**\\n\\n```ts\\nexpect(body.version).toBe('6.1.0');\\n```\\n\\nPackage version is now `6.8.0`. Pre-existing failure, but the sprint changed `server.ts` and this test is in scope — it should have been updated.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Security\\n\\nNo injection vectors, no credential exposure. The branch-delete endpoint already enforces `autonomous/*` prefix via `deleteBranchByName`. Memory writes are fire-and-forget inside `catch {}` — appropriate.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Test Coverage\\n\\nNew test files are strong: `memory.test.ts` covers all four new query params with edge cases, `branches.test.ts` and `sprints.test.ts` are comprehensive, and the two-cycle `memory-flow.test.ts` suite is the right integration anchor for the learning loop. The `gate-phase-memory.test.ts` suite is well-designed — it caught the real REJECT bug it was written to guard. The `sprint-framework.test.ts` additions correctly validate the new `recordReviewFindings` delegation.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Verdict\\n\\n```\\nVERDICT: REQUEST_CHANGES\\nSUMMARY: Strong feature set; one real correctness bug (REJECT mis-tagged APPROVE) and \\n         two meta.total reporting bugs need fixing before merge.\\nISSUES:\\n  - [CRITICAL] Gate verdict reads wrong phaseResult after runLlmPhase appends a new \\n    entry; REJECT sprint is tagged verdict:approve — packages/server/src/lib/phase-handlers.ts:655-662\\n  - [MAJOR] meta.total reflects post-slice count, not total filter matches — \\n    packages/server/src/routes/v5/dashboard-stubs.ts:168 and src/server/routes/memory.ts:330\\n  - [MINOR] readGateVerdict() implicit file-layout coupling in CycleLogger — \\n    packages/core/src/autonomous/cycle-logger.ts:134\\n  - [MINOR] Stale version assertion in integration test (6.1.0 vs 6.8.0) — \\n    tests/v5/server-integration.test.ts:22\\nSUGGESTIONS:\\n  - Pass verdict as a field on RunResult (or a wrapper) rather than re-reading disk\\n  - Consider adding a total-before-cap field (e.g., meta: { total: data.length, \\n    totalMatching: filtered.length }) so clients can distinguish cap vs filter count\\n  - The SSE handler extraction (handleSseConnection) is clean — good pattern to continue\\n  - listBranches() / deleteBranchByName() DRY refactor is textbook correct\\n```\\n\\n**Overall score: 3 / 5** — Good architectural progress and meaningful test additions, blocked by the REJECT-verdict bug which is a correctness issue in the core learning loop.\""],"majorFindings":["\"response\": \"`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`\\nThe uncommitted changes show 28 files with 1,767 net new lines. Running tests against both states reveals the sprint **reduced total failures from 44 → 31** (13 pre-existing tests fixed), but introduced 1 new regression and carries two `meta.total` correctness bugs across the new filtered memory endpoints.\\n`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Code Review — Sprint v9.0.1\\n\\n### Overall Correctness\\n\\nThe sprint's stated goals (gate-verdict memory, memory endpoint filters, cross-cycle audit propagation, SSE v5 stream, v5 branch endpoints, DRY branch-route refactor) are all present and structurally sound. The execute-phase cap-D fix (`Math.min(itemCount, ceilingParallelism)`) is correct and the test was updated to match the new optimistic parallelism behavior. Most of the work is solid.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Issues\\n\\n**[CRITICAL] REJECT verdict mis-tagged as APPROVE — `packages/server/src/lib/phase-handlers.ts:661`**\\n\\nThe new `runGatePhase` reads verdict from the sprint file _after_ `runLlmPhase` completes. Since `runLlmPhase` appends a new `phaseResult` entry, `.pop()` returns the LLM phase's freshly-written result — not any seeded historical entry. In the test fixture this overwritten entry contains the mock agent's response (typically empty/approve-shaped), so a sprint seeded with `\\\"REJECT: test coverage too low\\\"` is silently tagged `verdict:approve`. This is confirmed by `gate-phase-memory.test.ts` failing with `expected ['verdict:approve', …] to include 'verdict:reject'`. The response should be read from the `RunResult` returned directly by `runLlmPhase`, not scraped from disk.\\n\\n**[MAJOR] `meta.total` reports paginated count, not total matches — `packages/server/src/routes/v5/dashboard-stubs.ts` and `src/server/routes/memory.ts`**\\n\\n```ts\\n// dashboard-stubs.ts\\nconst data = filtered.slice(0, pageLimit);\\nreturn reply.send({ data, meta: { total: data.length } }); // ← data.length is sliced\\n```\\n\\n`meta.total` is supposed to tell the client how many entries _match_ the filters before pagination. After `.slice(0, pageLimit)`, `data.length` ≤ `pageLimit`, not the true match count. Any UI that renders \\\"showing X of Y\\\" or drives infinite scroll will misbehave. Should be `filtered.length`.\\n\\n**[MINOR] `readGateVerdict()` is tightly coupled to internal file layout — `packages/core/src/autonomous/cycle-logger.ts:134`**\\n\\n`CycleLogger` now reaches directly into `cycles/<id>/phases/gate.json` to read structured verdict data. This dual-write path (phase handler writes gate.json → cycle logger re-reads it) creates an implicit ordering contract. If the gate phase ever writes under a different path or format, the method silently returns `null` with no warning. Passing the verdict explicitly through `logCycleResult`'s parameter struct would be safer.\\n\\n**[MINOR] Version hardcode stale in integration test — `tests/v5/server-integration.test.ts:22`**\\n\\n```ts\\nexpect(body.version).toBe('6.1.0');\\n```\\n\\nPackage version is now `6.8.0`. Pre-existing failure, but the sprint changed `server.ts` and this test is in scope — it should have been updated.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Security\\n\\nNo injection vectors, no credential exposure. The branch-delete endpoint already enforces `autonomous/*` prefix via `deleteBranchByName`. Memory writes are fire-and-forget inside `catch {}` — appropriate.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Test Coverage\\n\\nNew test files are strong: `memory.test.ts` covers all four new query params with edge cases, `branches.test.ts` and `sprints.test.ts` are comprehensive, and the two-cycle `memory-flow.test.ts` suite is the right integration anchor for the learning loop. The `gate-phase-memory.test.ts` suite is well-designed — it caught the real REJECT bug it was written to guard. The `sprint-framework.test.ts` additions correctly validate the new `recordReviewFindings` delegation.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Verdict\\n\\n```\\nVERDICT: REQUEST_CHANGES\\nSUMMARY: Strong feature set; one real correctness bug (REJECT mis-tagged APPROVE) and \\n         two meta.total reporting bugs need fixing before merge.\\nISSUES:\\n  - [CRITICAL] Gate verdict reads wrong phaseResult after runLlmPhase appends a new \\n    entry; REJECT sprint is tagged verdict:approve — packages/server/src/lib/phase-handlers.ts:655-662\\n  - [MAJOR] meta.total reflects post-slice count, not total filter matches — \\n    packages/server/src/routes/v5/dashboard-stubs.ts:168 and src/server/routes/memory.ts:330\\n  - [MINOR] readGateVerdict() implicit file-layout coupling in CycleLogger — \\n    packages/core/src/autonomous/cycle-logger.ts:134\\n  - [MINOR] Stale version assertion in integration test (6.1.0 vs 6.8.0) — \\n    tests/v5/server-integration.test.ts:22\\nSUGGESTIONS:\\n  - Pass verdict as a field on RunResult (or a wrapper) rather than re-reading disk\\n  - Consider adding a total-before-cap field (e.g., meta: { total: data.length, \\n    totalMatching: filtered.length }) so clients can distinguish cap vs filter count\\n  - The SSE handler extraction (handleSseConnection) is clean — good pattern to continue\\n  - listBranches() / deleteBranchByName() DRY refactor is textbook correct\\n```\\n\\n**Overall score: 3 / 5** — Good architectural progress and meaningful test additions, blocked by the REJECT-verdict bug which is a correctness issue in the core learning loop.\""]}

{"cycleId":"6f5c77da-9770-4147-b2a3-afa230500e90","sprintVersion":"9.0.2","verdict":"APPROVE","rationale":"All 20 sprint items delivered and verified complete, tests pass, and the independent code review returned a 4/5 APPROVE verdict. The two MAJOR findings — tripled memory-filter logic and missing gate-phase test coverage — are legitimate maintenance debts but neither represents a correctness risk or regression vector that warrants blocking release. The core deliverables (memory schema, cross-cycle learning loop, dashboard wiring, parallelism bug fix) are confirmed correct and well-structured. Ship v9.0.2 now and ticket both follow-ups (extract shared applyMemoryFilters helper, add gate-phase integration tests) as mandatory items for the v9.1 sprint backlog.","criticalFindings":[],"majorFindings":["\"response\": \"`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`\\nThis sprint implements three interdependent concerns at once: (1) memory schema evolution (`key`, `line`, `metadata` fields), (2) new server-side API filters (`?type`, `?agentId`, `?since`), and (3) a live dashboard layer (SSE + stats bar). Reviewing at the intersection of all three is where integration bugs hide.\\n`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Code Review — Sprint v9.0.2\\n\\n### Overview\\n33 files changed, 2,141 insertions. The sprint delivers: memory API query filters, gate-verdict memory writing, `ReviewFinding` schema extension (`summary`/`line`/`metadata`), an execute-phase parallelism bug fix, per-workspace flywheel cache, and a substantially upgraded Memory dashboard (SSE live updates, type stats bar, new-entry highlighting).\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Correctness\\n\\n**Execute-phase parallelism fix** (`execute-phase.ts:371`) is correct. The old `cap = itemCount` silently overrode a user-set `maxParallelism: 1` for ≤3-item sprints. The new `Math.min(ceilingParallelism, itemCount)` respects the ceiling. Small change, real impact.\\n\\n**Gate-verdict memory writing** (`phase-handlers.ts:648–726`) correctly reads the pre-existing gate result so a re-run doesn't overwrite the original decision. The `ctx.cycleId ?? null` / `ctx.cycleId` (optional) usage is safe.\\n\\n**`ReviewFinding.message → summary`** rename propagates cleanly across `review-phase-handler.ts`, tests, and the type definition. The addition of optional `line?` and `fixSuggestion?` is backward-compatible.\\n\\n**Memory API filters** (`dashboard-stubs.ts`, `v6/index.ts`) are correct: `?since` uses `>=` (inclusive boundary — confirmed by the test that seeds `exact` entry), invalid dates fall through gracefully via `isNaN`, empty filter strings mean \\\"no filter.\\\"\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Code Quality Issues\\n\\n- **[MAJOR] Triple duplication of MEMORY_LIMIT + filter logic** — The same `MEMORY_LIMIT = 200`, `typeFilter/agentIdFilter/sinceMs` plumbing, and the `.filter(e => ...)` body appear identically in three files: `packages/server/src/routes/v5/dashboard-stubs.ts`, `packages/server/src/routes/v6/index.ts`, and `src/server/routes/memory.ts`. A single divergence here will create silent inconsistencies between routes. Extract a `applyMemoryFilters(entries, query)` helper. (`dashboard-stubs.ts:165–200`, `v6/index.ts:530–580`, `memory.ts:217+`)\\n\\n- **[MINOR] `extractFindingLines` duplicates core logic** — The comment in `phase-handlers.ts:645` says \\\"Mirrors `extractFindingsByLevel` in packages/core (not part of the public API).\\\" Two implementations means two places to drift. Either expose the core utility or tolerate the acknowledged duplication.\\n\\n- **[MINOR] `flywheelCacheByRoot` Map has no eviction** — Stale workspace entries accumulate indefinitely. Low risk (workspaces don't churn), but worth a `MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES` guard or periodic prune. (`dashboard-stubs.ts:22`)\\n\\n- **[MINOR] SSE reconnect is fixed 5 s, not exponential** — The comment says \\\"Exponential-ish backoff\\\" but the implementation is a flat `setTimeout(..., 5000)`. Misleading comment. (`memory/+page.svelte:143`)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Security\\n\\nNo SQL injection surface (JSONL/filesystem only). The `?workspaceId` → `getWorkspace()` call follows the same pattern used by other routes. `?since=not-a-date` is handled gracefully. No credentials exposed in diffs.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Test Coverage Gaps\\n\\n- **[MAJOR] `runGatePhase` memory-write path has zero new tests** — The gate phase now writes a `gate-verdict` entry; `extractFindingLines` is also new. Neither is covered by a test. The review-phase handler tests are exemplary; the gate handler deserves the same treatment. (`phase-handlers.ts:645–726`)\\n\\n- **[MINOR] `window.fetch` monkey-patch in `dashboard/pages/memory.html`** is not tested. If `loadMemoryBrowser` itself patches `fetch`, they compose incorrectly. A comment-level caveat would suffice given this is legacy JS, but it's a fragile pattern.\\n\\n- **[MINOR] SSE live-update path in `memory/+page.svelte`** (connect, reconnect, `load(true)` on `cycle.complete`) has no component tests.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Verdict\\n\\n**4 / 5 — Ship with follow-up tickets**\\n\\nThe sprint's core deliverables are correct and well-structured. The parallelism bug fix alone justifies the merge. The MEMORY_LIMIT/filter triplication is the only issue that will actively cause maintenance pain; the gate-phase test gap is the only place a regression could hide silently. Neither is a blocker, but both should be ticketed before v9.1 lands more memory consumers.\\n\\n```\\nVERDICT: APPROVE (with follow-up)\\nSUMMARY: Correct sprint delivery; one structural quality debt and one test gap to chase\\nISSUES:\\n  - [MAJOR] MEMORY_LIMIT + filter logic duplicated in 3 route files — extract shared helper\\n            dashboard-stubs.ts:165, v6/index.ts:530, memory.ts:217\\n  - [MAJOR] runGatePhase memory-write path (extractFindingLines + writeMemoryEntry) untested\\n            phase-handlers.ts:645–726\\n  - [MINOR] flywheelCacheByRoot Map has no eviction / size cap — dashboard-stubs.ts:22\\n  - [MINOR] SSE reconnect comment says \\\"exponential\\\" but is flat 5 s — memory/+page.svelte:143\\nSUGGESTIONS:\\n  - Move applyMemoryFilters() + MEMORY_LIMIT into a shared @agentforge/core utility so all\\n    three route files import rather than re-implement\\n  - Add gate-phase integration test: seed a sprint with a review result, run runGatePhase,\\n    assert gate-verdict.jsonl contains the right verdict tag + rationale slice\\n```\""]}

Ship it.","criticalFindings":[],"majorFindings":["\"response\": \"`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`\\nThis sprint wires `gateVerdict` end-to-end across three distinct layers: the runtime type system (`CycleResult`), the memory persistence path (`CycleLogger`), and the audit prompt builder (`AuditPhaseHandler`). This layered propagation pattern — where structured data flows down from the error throw site all the way into future LLM context — is the key architectural pattern to evaluate here.\\n`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Code Review: Sprint v9.1.0 (v9.0.2 commit)\\n\\n### Overall Correctness\\n\\nThe sprint delivered all major items claimed in the commit message. The `gateVerdict` field propagates cleanly from `GateRejectedError` → `CycleRunner.buildResult()` → `CycleLogger` → memory JSONL. The `AuditPhaseHandler.buildGateVerdictDescription()` helper correctly prefers structured `GateVerdictMetadata` and falls back to the legacy plain summary. The org-graph multi-source edge merge with deduplication via `edgeSet` is algorithmically correct and handles the symmetric case (`can_delegate_to` ↔ `reports_to`) without producing duplicates. The `searchRoutes` dedup fix in `index.ts` is the right solution to the `FST_ERR_DUPLICATED_ROUTE` problem.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Code Quality\\n\\n**MINOR — Dead comment block in `flywheel/+page.svelte` (line ~107):**\\n```ts\\nif (hasPrevData) {\\n  // Trend arrows visible after second+ load\\n}\\n```\\nThis block has no body — the actual `getTrend()` function handles this logic separately. Leaving an empty `if` block with a misleading comment is confusing noise.\\n\\n**MINOR — Export alias inconsistency in `src/autonomous/index.ts`:**\\n```ts\\ntype GateVerdictMemoryWriter as GatePhaseMemoryWriter,\\n```\\nThe underlying module uses `GateVerdictMemoryWriter`; the public index re-exports it as `GatePhaseMemoryWriter`. This split identity will confuse consumers who try to match the name against the source file. Either name consistently, or drop the alias.\\n\\n**MINOR — CSS class namespace workaround is implicit:**  \\nThe filter pill for \\\"active\\\" branches was renamed from `.pill.active` to `.pill.active-pill` to avoid collision with the `class:active` filter state. This is correct but silent — a future dev modifying the pills will hit the same trap without understanding why.\\n\\n**MINOR — `formatValueFull` referenced in new `copyValue()` helper** in `memory/+page.svelte` — function is pre-existing (not in diff), so the reference is safe, but a quick search confirms it exists. No bug, just worth checking.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Security\\n\\nNo security concerns found. The `branches/+page.svelte` `deleteAllStale` function uses `encodeURIComponent(branch.name)` before inserting branch names in the URL path. The `agents.ts` server route validates all YAML fields with explicit type guards before exposing them. No SQL, no eval, no credentials in diff.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Test Coverage\\n\\n**Good:** `audit-phase-handler.test.ts` adds 80+ lines testing all `buildGateVerdictDescription` paths exhaustively — rich metadata, legacy entries, missing rationale, empty findings. The three new `cycle-logger` tests cover `APPROVE`, `REJECT`, and `undefined` verdict cases cleanly.\\n\\n**Gap — `run.ts` `onChunk` streaming:** The new `onChunk` callback emitting to `globalStream` has no test coverage. The risk is low (wires a callback into an existing streaming runtime), but an integration or unit test verifying that chunks arrive on the SSE channel before the `done` event would be worth adding.\\n\\n**Gap — `modelIdToTierLabel` in `runner/+page.svelte`:** Simple string-matching helper with no tests. Edge cases (empty string, unknown model ID, model IDs like `claude-3-5-sonnet`) should be verified; currently the default `return 'Sonnet'` silently absorbs unknowns.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Bugs Worth Rejecting For\\n\\nNone. All pre-existing failures in the 30-failure count are acknowledged as pre-existing.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Suggestions (Non-blocking)\\n\\n- Consider parallelising `deleteAllStale` with `Promise.allSettled()` — the sequential `for...of` loop blocks on each DELETE, making bulk cleanup on large branch lists noticeably slow.\\n- The `flywheel.html` static page and `flywheel/+page.svelte` now have parallel memory card rendering logic. If the static page is transitional/deprecated, a comment noting its planned removal would help.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Verdict\\n\\n```\\nVERDICT: APPROVE\\nSUMMARY: gateVerdict wiring is correct and well-tested; UI improvements are\\n         extensive and safe; two minor export/naming nits and a dead comment\\n         block, but nothing blocking.\\n\\nISSUES:\\n  - [MINOR] Dead empty if-block in flywheel/+page.svelte ~line 107\\n  - [MINOR] GateVerdictMemoryWriter re-exported as GatePhaseMemoryWriter in\\n            src/autonomous/index.ts — split identity, pick one name\\n  - [MINOR] run.ts onChunk streaming path has no test coverage\\n\\nSUGGESTIONS:\\n  - Parallelise deleteAllStale with Promise.allSettled for better UX on\\n    large stale-branch lists\\n  - Add unit tests for modelIdToTierLabel edge cases (empty string, unknown\\n    model ID)\\n  - Note flywheel.html deprecation if the static page is being phased out\\n    in favour of the Svelte route\\n```\\n\\n**Overall: 4 / 5** — Ship it. The core logic is sound, test coverage on the new backend paths is thorough, and the UX improvements are clean. The minor nits don't block merge.\""]}
